title: Enterospermum bernierianum
intro: Enterospermum bernierianum, also known as the "Mao Kiep" plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. Traditional medicine has long valued Enterospermum bernierianum for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which are said to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Enterospermum bernierianum include limonoids, flavonoids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Enterospermum bernierianum is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Enterospermum bernierianum is a genus of extinct, ancient flowering plants.
